resolution_requirement: Replace only after FLUXNET or other independent ecosystem-function validation artifacts support the claim.
context: Compound heat and water stress increasingly disrupt terrestrial ecosystems, yet the climate precursors that separate recoverable vegetation anomalies from persistent vegetation stress remain difficult to identify across regions and biomes. Here we propose an interpretable artificial intelligence framework that links lagged atmospheric and land-surface conditions from ERA5/ERA5-Land to quality-filtered MODIS vegetation-index anomalies, with independent evaluation against FLUXNET ecosystem flux observations. The analysis is designed to discover whether recurrent combinations of temperature, soil moisture, precipitation deficit and vapour pressure deficit precede extreme negative vegetation anomalies at global scale. RESULT_REQUIRED. Temporal holdout, leave-region-out validation, baseline climate indices and site-level flux checks will be used to determine whether the discovered precursors are predictive, geographically transferable and ecologically meaningful. RESULT_REQUIRED. These results would provide an evidence-based route for early warning of vegetation stress and for mechanistic interpretation of compound climate extremes. RESULT_REQUIRED.

context: The study is intended to make three contributions. First, it will provide a reproducible global map of vegetation-stress events derived from satellite anomaly records. Second, it will identify and test multi-variable climate precursors of those events, including compound heat-drought and atmospheric-demand states. Third, it will evaluate whether the discovered precursors correspond to independent reductions in ecosystem function at FLUXNET sites. RESULT_REQUIRED.
